4 Question 2

# pak::pak("thomasgstewart/ds6021b")

require(ds6021b)

Loading required package: ds6021b

R1 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 0)
plot(R1[,1], f1(R1), xlab = "x1", ylab = "y", type = "l")
legend("topleft", legend = "x2 = -0.5 & x3 = 0", box.lty = 0)







R2 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 0)
plot(R2[,1], f2(R2), xlab = "x1", ylab = "y", type = "l")
legend("topleft", legend = "x2 = -0.5 & x3 = 0", box.lty = 0)







R3 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 0)

5 Question 3

R1_0 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 0)
R1_1 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 1)
plot(R1_0[,1], f1(R1_0), xlab = "x1", ylab = "y", ylim = c(-1,4),type = "l")
lines(R1_1[,1], f1(R1_1), xlab = "x1", ylab = "y", type = "l", col = "red")
legend("topleft", legend = "x2 = -0.5 & x3 = 0 or 1", box.lty = 0)

R2_1 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 1)

lines(R3_1[,1], f3(R3_1), xlab = "x1", ylab = "y", type = "l", col = "red")
legend("topleft", legend = "x2 = -0.5 & x3 = 0 or 1", box.lty = 0)







R4_0 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 0)

R5_0 <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-3,3,by=0.01), x2 = -0.5, x3 = 0)

6 Question 4
For the most part, a visual glance indicates that x3 with value 1 has higher y values than x3 with value 0. Although the results do vary in the final curve/outcome, the general shape of these plots are retained.

title: 4  Homework 2
text:
5 Question 1
#pak::pak("thomasgstewart/ds6021c")
library(ds6021b)
library(ds6021c)

set.seed(23583)

d <- external_data(n=1000)

for (fi in c("f1", "f2", "f3", "f4", "f5")) {
    par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
    for (grp in c(0, 1)) {
        idx <- d$x3 == grp
        preds <- get(fi)(d)[idx]
        obs <- d$y[idx]
        plot(preds, obs,
            xlab = "Predicted", ylab = "Observed",
            main = paste0(fi, " (x3 = ", grp, ")"),
            pch = 16, col = "#00000030", asp = 1)
        
        lines(lowess(preds, obs), lwd = 2, col = "blue")
        abline(0, 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
    }
    par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}

title: 4  Homework 2
text:
7.1 MSE

set.seed(1)
d <- external_data(1000)

mse_vals <- sapply(c("f1","f2","f3","f4","f5"), function(fi) {
  mean((get(fi)(d) - d$y)^2)
})

plot(1:5, mse_vals,
        ylab = "Mean Squared Error",
        main = "Expected Squared Error by Model",
        col = "steelblue",
        ylim = c(0,max(mse_vals)),
        pch = 16,
        cex = 1.5,
        xaxt = "n")
axis(1, 1:5, c("f1","f2","f3","f4","f5"))

title: 4  Homework 2
text:
7.2 MAE

mae_vals <- sapply(c("f1","f2","f3","f4","f5"), function(fi) {
  quantile(abs(get(fi)(d) - d$y), probs = 0.9)
})

plot(1:5, mae_vals,
        ylab = "Mean Squared Error",
        main = "Expected Absolute Error by Model",
        col = "steelblue",
        ylim = c(0,max(mae_vals)),
        pch = 16,
        cex = 1.5,
        xaxt = "n")
axis(1, 1:5, c("f1","f2","f3","f4","f5"))

title: 4  Homework 2
text:
7.3 q90

q90_vals <- sapply(c("f1","f2","f3","f4","f5"), function(fi) {
  mean(abs(get(fi)(d) - d$y))
})

plot(1:5, q90_vals,
        ylab = "90th Quantile",
        main = "Expected 90th Quantile by Model",
        col = "steelblue",
        ylim = c(0,max(q90_vals)),
        pch = 16,
        cex = 1.5,
        xaxt = "n")
axis(1, 1:5, c("f1","f2","f3","f4","f5"))
